name: AMN HEALTHCARE SERVICES INC
business: AMN Healthcare provides total talent solutions for the healthcare sector, including staffing, talent optimization strategies, and technology solutions. The company offers temporary and permanent placement of nurses, physicians, allied health professionals, and healthcare leaders. Its services range from traditional staffing to managed services programs, vendor management systems, and language interpretation.

exposures.sentence: We are the leader and innovator in total talent solutions for the healthcare sector in the United States, and provide staffing, talent optimization strategies, and technology solutions to support caregivers and patient care.
ShiftWise Flex is also integrated with AMN Passport, our top clinician-rated mobile application with more than 340,000 registered users as of January 2026.

We are increasing investments in automation and AI-enabled technology to improve efficiency and speed across our operations, with positive early results in functions including recruiting, credentialing and candidate engagement.
Security breaches and cybersecurity incidents could compromise our information and systems adversely affecting our business operations and reputation subject us to substantial liabilities.

As we incorporate AI and machine learning into our business there are uncertainties in the legal regulatory regime relating to AI that may require significant resources to modify and maintain our business practices to comply with U.S. and non-U.S. laws, the nature of which cannot be determined at this time.

tamClaims.claim: The Association of American Medical Colleges estimates a nationwide physician shortage up to 86,000 by 2036.
tamClaims.sentence: The Association of American Medical Colleges estimates a nationwide physician shortage up to 86,000 by 2036.

tamClaims.claim: World Health Organization estimates a worldwide shortage of 4.5 million by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: In nursing, the World Health Organization estimates a worldwide shortage of 4.5 million by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 900,000 registered nurses are anticipated to leave the workforce by the end of 2027.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Council of State Boards of Nursing, approximately 900,000 registered nurses are anticipated to leave the workforce by the end of 2027.

tamClaims.claim: National health spending projected to grow at average annual rates of 5.6% and 5.3% from 2026-2027 and 2028-2033, respectively; Medicare spending growth projected at 8.9% and 7.4% for the same periods.
tamClaims.sentence: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services projected to grow at average annual rates of 5.6% and 5.3% from 2026-2027 and 2028-2033, respectively, while Medicare spending growth is projected to increase to average annual rates of 8.9% and 7.4% for the same time periods, respectively.

tamClaims.claim: Number of adults age 65+ grew 13% from 2020 to 2024 and projected to grow 31% between 2022 and 2035.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, the number of adults age 65 or older grew by 13.0% from 2020 to 2024 and is projected to grow an estimated 31% between 2022 and 2035.
